reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 3 gates passed. Gate 1: Handler actively registered in rpc.go (non-test callers exist). Gate 2: Entry point is P2P peers (UNTRUSTED per trust_assumptions). Gate 3: Fusaka-specific PeerDAS deprecation requirement, in scope. An attacker can trigger this via P2P network by sending blob_sidecars_by_root requests after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + 4096 epochs, causing the node to serve legacy RPC requests instead of rejecting them, delaying PeerDAS migration and enabling resource exhaustion. Severity calibrated against Prysm's 31% market share (exceeds 5% Medium threshold).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: blob_sidecars_by_root must be fully deprecated after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS (EIP-7594 PeerDAS transition)
